person.name: Conde Pedro Vélez, VII Señor de Guevara Linaje de Vela, Conde de Alava, descendiente de la Casa Real de Navarra, de los Duques de Vasconia, de Aquitana y de Bretaña

nameForms.fullText: Conde Pedro Vélez, VII Señor de Guevara Linaje de Vela, Conde de Alava, descendiente de la Casa Real de Navarra, de los Duques de Vasconia, de Aquitana y de Bretaña

spouse.name: María Sáenz de Salcedo, Señora de Ayala Linaje de Ayala, descendiente de las Casas Reales de Navarra, de Aragón y de León y de los Señores de Vizcaya

km.Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, España: តូឡិដូ, ខាស្ទីលឡាលា ម៉ាnចា, អេស្ប៉ាញ
km.Álava, Basque Country, Spain: aឡាវ៉ា, អេuស្កាឌិ, អេស្ប៉ាញ

zh.Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, España: 西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈托萊多
zh.Álava, Basque Country, Spain: 西班牙艾尤斯卡迪阿拉瓦
zh.lifeSummary: 當Conde Pedro Vélez, VII Señor de Guevara Linaje de Vela, Conde de Alava, descendiente de la Casa Real de Navarra, de los Duques de Vasconia, de Aquitana y de Bretaña於1157年出生在西班牙艾尤斯卡迪阿拉瓦時，他的父親Juan Velaz de Guevara17歲，母親María Álvarez de Arazuri23歲。他和María Sáenz de Salcedo, Señora de Ayala Linaje de Ayala, descendiente de las Casas Reales de Navarra, de Aragón y de León y de los Señores de Vizcaya至少有1個兒子。

zh-hans.Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, España: 西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈托萊多
zh-hans.Álava, Basque Country, Spain: 西班牙艾尤斯卡迪阿拉瓦
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Conde Pedro Vélez, VII Señor de Guevara Linaje de Vela, Conde de Alava, descendiente de la Casa Real de Navarra, de los Duques de Vasconia, de Aquitana y de Bretaña于1157年出生在西班牙艾尤斯卡迪阿拉瓦时，他的父亲Juan Velaz de Guevara17岁，母亲María Álvarez de Arazuri23岁。他和María Sáenz de Salcedo, Señora de Ayala Linaje de Ayala, descendiente de las Casas Reales de Navarra, de Aragón y de León y de los Señores de Vizcaya至少有1个儿子。

nameMeanings.name: VII
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Haitian: ornamental or topographic name, based on the Haitian Creole spelling of French ville (compare St. Vil ).

French and possibly also Haitian: shortened form of Ville, a habitational name from (La) Ville, the name of several places in various parts of France named with ville ‘settlement’. This surname is rare in France. It was brought to the US mainly from Haiti (see also 1 above).

variants.name: St. Vil
meanings: Haitian (mainly Saint-Vil):
ornamental name composed of two common French (and Haitian Creole) surname elements, saint ‘holy’ and vil ‘settlement’ (see Vil ); compare Vilsaint .

alternatively, perhaps a shortened form of French Saintville, a habitational name from Sainville (Eure-et-Loir), or an altered form of French Sainthuile, itself a respelled habitational name from Sainte-Olle (Nord). Compare also Saintvil and Sainvil .

nameMeanings.name: Señor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Andres, Blanca, Don Juan, Felipe, Valeria.
Spanish (Señor): nickname from the title señor‘mister’.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Guevara
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Juan, Luis, Jesus, Francisco, Jorge, Ana, Miguel, Julio, Manuel, Roberto.

Basque: Castilianized form of Gebara 2, a habitational name from a place so called in the province of Araba/Álava. The origin and meaning of the placename are uncertain; it is recorded in the form Gebala by the geographer Ptolemy in the 2nd century AD . Compare Gevara , Guebara , Guevarra , and Guevera .

variants.name: Guevera
meanings: Hispanic: altered form of Basque   Guevara  .

variants.name: Gevara
meanings: Hispanic: altered form of Basque   Guevara   or of its original form   Gebara  .

variants.name: Gebara
meanings: Basque: habitational name from a place so called in the province of Araba/Álava (see   Guevara  ). This surname is very rare in Spain.
Arabic (Lebanon and Syria): variant of   Jabara  . Bearers of this surname are both Christians and Muslims.

variants.name: Guebara
meanings: Basque: variant of Guevara , Castilianized form of Gebara 2. The surname Guebara is virtually non-existing in Spain.

variants.name: Guevarra
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Andres, Artemio, Carlos, Edgardo, Jesus, Manuel, Adelaida, Adoracion, Aida, Altagracia, Aristedes.
Hispanic (mainly Philippines): altered form of Basque   Guevara  .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Vela,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Carlos, Raul, Manuel, Ricardo, Roberto, Ruben, Jesus, Luis, Guadalupe, Ramon.
Spanish: possibly from a pre-Roman personal name, Vela (compare Basque bela, vela ‘raven’). More likely perhaps, it may be a byname from vela meaning either ‘sail’ or ‘watchman’ (compare 4 below).
Spanish: habitational name from a place called Vela (in Córdoba province) or La Vela (in Murcia), probably named with vela ‘guard’.

variants.name: Veleta
meanings: Czech: from a derivative of compound personal names based on the Slavic element vel ‘big, great’, also ‘significant’, e.g. Veleslav.
Hispanic (Mexico): possibly a diminutive of   Vela  .

variants.name: Velez
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Luis, Juan, Pedro, Manuel, Angel, Jorge, Rafael, Francisco, Miguel, Ramon.
Spanish (Vélez): patronymic from the personal name Vela .
Spanish (Vélez): habitational name from any of various places in Andalusia called Vélez. Compare Veles .

nameMeanings.name: Conde
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Manuel, Luis, Juan, Pedro, Raul, Jorge, Ruben, Angel, Cesar, Fernando.  Portuguese Goncalo, Manoel.
Spanish and Portuguese: nickname from the title of rank conde ‘count’, a derivative of Latin comes, comitis ‘companion’. See Conte .
English (Shropshire): variant of Cundy , a cognate of 3 below, or Cound, a habitational name from Cound in Shropshire.

variants.name: Conte
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Mario, Angelo, Salvatore, Antonio, Rocco, Carmine, Pasquale, Luigi, Silvio, Silverio, Giuseppe, Sal, Alfonso, Biagio, Carlo, Emilio, Fernando, Gerardo.

Italian: from the title of rank conte ‘count’, from Latin comes, genitive comitis ‘companion’. Probably in this sense (and in the Late Latin sense of ‘traveling companion’), it was a medieval personal name. As a title it was no doubt applied ironically as a nickname for someone with airs and graces or simply as a metonymic occupational name for someone in the service of a count. Compare Lo Conte .
Spanish: variant of   Conde   ‘count’ (see also 1 above).

variants.name: Candee
meanings: Americanized form of French Condé (see Conde ) or, alternatively, Candé, a habitational name from Candé in Maine-et-Loire, or from Candé-sur-Beauvron in Loire-et-Cher, or southern French Condy .
Alternatively, perhaps an altered form of English Condy , a cognate of the name Candé in 1 above.

History: The Candees trace their origin to Zaccheus Cande(e), who in the middle of the 17th century resided in the New Haven County, CT, and his probable father John Condy alias Conde. The name of the latter is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors as Jean/John Candée/Cande/Condé and in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America as John Conde. See also   Conde  .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Alava,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Spanish (Álava): habitational name from the province of Araba/Álava in the Basque Country.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Casa
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Antonio, Angelo, Camillo, Ciro, Dante, Domenic, Gaetano, Gennaro, Giovanna, Guido, Luigi, Onofrio.  Spanish Blanca, Ernesto, Carlos, Alicia, Esperanza, Martinez, Miguel, Rafael, Adriana, Alberto, Alvarez, Benito.

Italian, Spanish, Catalan, Portuguese, and southern French: topographic name from casa ‘house’ (from Latin casa ‘hut, cottage, cabin’), perhaps originally denoting the occupier of the most distinguished house in a village.
Italian: from a short form of the personal name Benincasa, an omen or well-wishing name meaning ‘welcome in (our) house’.

variants.name: Casilla
meanings: Spanish: habitational name from any of numerous places called Casilla, from a diminutive of casa ‘house’ (see Casa ).

variants.name: Casillas
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Jesus, Juan, Carlos, Ramon, Francisco, Manuel, Jorge, Mario, Miguel, Ruben, Guadalupe.
Spanish: habitational name from any of numerous places so called, especially Casillas in Avila province, from the plural of a diminutive of casa ‘house’ (see Casa ).

variants.name: Casino
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Alfredo, Cesar, Cristina, Domingo, Efren, Epifania, Herminio, Margarita, Miguel, Pacita, Rolando, Romaldo.  Italian Salvatore, Aldo, Leonardo, Pasquale.
Italian: from a pet form of the personal name Caso, itself from a diminutive of the omen or well-wishing name Benincasa (see Casa ).
Catalan: topographic name or nickname referring to casino ‘casino’, a loanword from Italian casino ‘little house’.

variants.name: Caso
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Luis, Carlos, Alfonso, Jose, Roberto, Diego, Eduardo, Orlando, Alfredo, Americo, Angelina, Evelio.  Italian Sal, Antonio, Angelo, Carmine, Carlo, Carmelo, Ciro, Damiano, Enrico, Gasper, Giuseppe, Margherita.
Spanish: habitational name from Caso in Oviedo (Asturias).
Italian (southern, mainly Sicily): from a masculinized form of the personal name Casa, a short form of the omen or well-wishing name Benincasa (see Casa ).

variants.name: Cason
meanings: English (Norfolk and Suffolk): habitational name for someone from Cawston in Norfolk; the form of the surname reflects the local pronunciation of the placename, which is from the Old Norse personal name Kalfr + Old English tūn ‘settlement’.
Italian (Veneto): augmentative form of   Casa  .

variants.name: Casas
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Luis, Carlos, Francisco, Raul, Roberto, Ricardo, Ruben, Armando, Guadalupe.  Portuguese Joao, Ligia, Paulo.
Spanish and Portuguese: plural form of   Casa  , frequent as a habitational and topographic name. Compare   De Casas  .
Catalan: Castilianized form of   Cases  .

variants.name: Casella
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Carmine, Angelo, Salvatore, Alessandra, Attilio, Carmelo, Rocco, Vito, Alfio, Antonio, Carlo, Carmello.
Italian: diminutive of   Casa  .

variants.name: Cases
meanings: Catalan: plural form of   Casa  , frequent as a habitational and topographic name.

variants.name: Casola
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Sal, Antonella, Carlo, Franco, Gaetano, Giovanna, Lelio, Salvatore.  Spanish Adolfo, Carolina, Jaime, Leopoldo, Luis, Mario, Pura, Ramona, Rigoberto, Zoraida.
Italian (southern; Càsola): diminutive of Casa .
Catalan (Casolà): nickname from casolà ‘inhabitant of a casal’ (see Casale ) or ‘home-loving’.

variants.name: Casazza
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Marco, Dino, Domenico, Enzo, Guido, Renzo, Silvio.
Italian: habitational name from a place called Casazza, for example in Sicily, or a topographic name from casazza, a pejorative or augmentative of casa ‘house’ (see Casa ).

nameMeanings.name: Real
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Juan, Guillermo, Jose, Carlos, Francisco, Luis, Ricardo, Alberto, Armando, Blanca, Lourdes, Manuel.  Portuguese Joao, Joaquim.

Spanish, Catalan, Portuguese, and Galician: habitational name from any of numerous places called Real; those in Galicia (Spain) and Portugal being named from real ‘royal’ or as variant of Rial , while those in southern Spain and Catalonia are named in part from real meaning ‘encampment, rural property’ (Arabic raḥāl ‘farmhouse, cabin’). Compare Del Real .

Spanish; French (Réal): from Spanish and Old French real ‘royal’, hence a nickname for someone who behaved in a regal manner, or an occupational name for someone in the service of the king, or a topographic name for someone who lived on a land belonging to the king. As a French name it may also be a habitational name from Réal (Pyrénées-Orientales), a place called for having been part of a royal domain (compare Reaux ).

variants.name: Reaux
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Camille.
French (Réaux): variant of Réal (see Real 2), as a habitational name referring in part to Réaux in Charente-Maritime.
Altered form, under the French influence in LA, of Spanish Riado, unexplained. This form of the surname has reportedly developed via the intermediate form Rieux 2.

variants.name: Rial
meanings: Galician: habitational name from any of numerous places called Rial in Galicia (Spain), derived from vulgar Latin rius ‘river’.
English (East Yorkshire): variant of   Ryall  .

variants.name: Reale
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Gaetano, Rocco, Antonio, Cosmo, Gino, Giuseppe, Pasquale, Agostino, Angelo, Carmela, Carmello.
Italian: from reale ‘royal’, used perhaps as a nickname for someone who behaved in a regal manner.
English and Irish: variant of   Real  .

variants.name: Reales
meanings: Spanish: plural form of   Real  .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Navarra,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Sal, Edgardo, Guido, Santo, Vito, Angelo, Annamarie, Antonio, Benedicto, Camillo, Dante, Emilio, Fausto, Gaetano, Giuseppe, Mario, Romulo, Sergio.  Spanish Agustin, Herlinda, Manuel, Solita.

Spanish, Portuguese, Italian, and Jewish (Sephardic): habitational name from Navarre (Castilian Navarra, Basque Nafarroa, French Navarre), a largely Basque-speaking region now divided between Spain and France, but in the Middle Ages an independent Basque kingdom. The name of the region derives from Basque naba ‘plain next to mountains’. Compare Navarre .

variants.name: Navarrete
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Carlos, Jesus, Pedro, Raul, Luis, Miguel, Roberto, Salvador, Francisco.

Spanish: habitational name from any of the places in La Rioja, Aragon, and the Basque Country named in Castilian Navarrete and in Basque Nafarrate (the latter in Araba/Álava province), of Basque origin (compare   Navarra   and   Nafarrate  ).
Basque: Castilianized form of Nafarrate or Nafarrete, cognates of 1.

variants.name: Navarro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Carlos, Jesus, Luis, Ramon, Francisco, Miguel, Rafael, Jorge, Pedro.
Spanish, Italian, and Jewish (Sephardic): habitational name for someone from Navarre (see   Navarra  ).

variants.name: Nava
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Jesus, Carlos, Luis, Pedro, Manuel, Alfredo, Francisco, Ruben, Javier, Enrique.

Spanish, Catalan, and Asturian-Leonese: habitational name from any of numerous places called Nava, named with nava ‘treeless plateau’, a word of pre-Roman origin, most probably from Basque naba ‘plain next to mountains’ (see Navarra ).

variants.name: Navarre
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Jacques, Anatole, Angelle, Camille, Marcelle.
French: habitational name from Navarre (see   Navarra  ).

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: los
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Jozef, Stanislaw, Tadeusz, Andrej, Bogdan, Czeslaw, Dorota, Edyta, Iwan, Jerzy, Ryszard, Wladyslaw.
Polish (Łoś): nickname for a large, ungainly person, from Polish łoś ‘elk’.
Dutch: nickname from Middle Dutch losch ‘squinting, cross-eyed’; ‘one-eyed’.

nameMeanings.name: Duques
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French:
habitational name, with fused preposition and definite article du ‘from the’, for someone from Le Quet in the Vosges or Quet-en-Beaumont in Isère.
ironic nickname from a diminutive of duc ‘duke’. Compare Duquette .

variants.name: Duquette
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Lucien, Armand, Camille, Adrien, Arsene, Fernand, Jacques, Marcel, Normand, Raoul, Achille, Amie.
Altered form of French Duquet , reflecting the Canadian and American French practice of sounding the final -t. Compare Dukette .
History: Denis Duquet from France married Catherine Gauthier in Quebec City, QC, in 1638.

German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bretaña
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Guadalupe, Jesus, Juan, Juana, Pedro, Enrique, Eugenio, Francisco, Luis, Manuel.
Basque: Castilianized form of a habitational name from Erretana (in Castilian: Retana) in Araba/Álava province, Basque Country (Spain).

nameMeanings.name: Conde
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Manuel, Luis, Juan, Pedro, Raul, Jorge, Ruben, Angel, Cesar, Fernando.  Portuguese Goncalo, Manoel.
Spanish and Portuguese: nickname from the title of rank conde ‘count’, a derivative of Latin comes, comitis ‘companion’. See Conte .
English (Shropshire): variant of Cundy , a cognate of 3 below, or Cound, a habitational name from Cound in Shropshire.

variants.name: Conte
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Mario, Angelo, Salvatore, Antonio, Rocco, Carmine, Pasquale, Luigi, Silvio, Silverio, Giuseppe, Sal, Alfonso, Biagio, Carlo, Emilio, Fernando, Gerardo.

Italian: from the title of rank conte ‘count’, from Latin comes, genitive comitis ‘companion’. Probably in this sense (and in the Late Latin sense of ‘traveling companion’), it was a medieval personal name. As a title it was no doubt applied ironically as a nickname for someone with airs and graces or simply as a metonymic occupational name for someone in the service of a count. Compare Lo Conte .
Spanish: variant of   Conde   ‘count’ (see also 1 above).

variants.name: Candee
meanings: Americanized form of French Condé (see Conde ) or, alternatively, Candé, a habitational name from Candé in Maine-et-Loire, or from Candé-sur-Beauvron in Loire-et-Cher, or southern French Condy .
Alternatively, perhaps an altered form of English Condy , a cognate of the name Candé in 1 above.

History: The Candees trace their origin to Zaccheus Cande(e), who in the middle of the 17th century resided in the New Haven County, CT, and his probable father John Condy alias Conde. The name of the latter is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors as Jean/John Candée/Cande/Condé and in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America as John Conde. See also   Conde  .

nameMeanings.name: Pedro
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Greek. See Peter .

variants.name: Peter
meanings: form of the name of the best-known of all Christ's apostles, traditionally regarded as the founder of the Christian Church. The name derives, via Latin, from Greek petros ‘stone, rock’. This is used as a translation of the Aramaic byname Cephas, given to the apostle Simon son of Jona, to distinguish him from another of the same name ( Simon Zelotes ). ‘When Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone’ (John 1:42). According to Matthew 16:17–18, Christ says more explicitly, ‘Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona … thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church’. The name has been in continuous use since the Middle Ages.

nameMeanings.name: Vélez,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Luis, Juan, Pedro, Manuel, Angel, Jorge, Rafael, Francisco, Miguel, Ramon.
Spanish (Vélez): patronymic from the personal name Vela .
Spanish (Vélez): habitational name from any of various places in Andalusia called Vélez. Compare Veles .

variants.name: Veles
meanings: Hispanic (mainly Mexico): altered form of Spanish Vélez (see Velez ).
Slovak: unexplained.

variants.name: Vela
meanings: Of modern origin and uncertain derivation, possibly based on   Selma   or   Thelma .

historicEvents.description: The Christian territory controlled the Kingdoms of Aragon, Navarre, and Castile.

en.lifeSummary: When Conde Pedro Vélez, VII Señor de Guevara Linaje de Vela, Conde de Alava, descendiente de la Casa Real de Navarra, de los Duques de Vasconia, de Aquitana y de Bretaña was born in 1157, in Álava, Basque Country, Spain, his father, Juan Velaz de Guevara, was 17 and his mother, María Álvarez de Arazuri, was 23. He had at least 1 son with María Sáenz de Salcedo, Señora de Ayala Linaje de Ayala, descendiente de las Casas Reales de Navarra, de Aragón y de León y de los Señores de Vizcaya.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Conde Pedro Vélez, VII Señor de Guevara Linaje de Vela, Conde de Alava, descendiente de la Casa Real de Navarra, de los Duques de Vasconia, de Aquitana y de Bretaña est né en 1157, à Álava, Euskadi, Espagne, son père, Juan Velaz de Guevara, avait 17 ans et sa mère, María Álvarez de Arazuri, avait 23 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec María Sáenz de Salcedo, Señora de Ayala Linaje de Ayala, descendiente de las Casas Reales de Navarra, de Aragón y de León y de los Señores de Vizcaya.

de.lifeSummary: Als Conde Pedro Vélez, VII Señor de Guevara Linaje de Vela, Conde de Alava, descendiente de la Casa Real de Navarra, de los Duques de Vasconia, de Aquitana y de Bretaña 1157 in Álava, Euskadi, Spanien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Juan Velaz de Guevara 17 und seine Mutter María Álvarez de Arazuri 23. Er hatte mit María Sáenz de Salcedo, Señora de Ayala Linaje de Ayala, descendiente de las Casas Reales de Navarra, de Aragón y de León y de los Señores de Vizcaya mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Conde Pedro Vélez, VII Señor de Guevara Linaje de Vela, Conde de Alava, descendiente de la Casa Real de Navarra, de los Duques de Vasconia, de Aquitana y de Bretaña è nato il nel 1157 nel Álava, Euskadi, Spagna, il suo padre, Juan Velaz de Guevara, aveva 17 anni e il suo madre, María Álvarez de Arazuri, aveva 23 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con María Sáenz de Salcedo, Señora de Ayala Linaje de Ayala, descendiente de las Casas Reales de Navarra, de Aragón y de León y de los Señores de Vizcaya.

ja.lifeSummary: Conde Pedro Vélez, VII Señor de Guevara Linaje de Vela, Conde de Alava, descendiente de la Casa Real de Navarra, de los Duques de Vasconia, de Aquitana y de Bretañaが1157年にスペインエウスカジーaラヴァで生まれた時，父Juan Velaz de Guevaraは17歳，母María Álvarez de Arazuriは23歳であった。彼はMaría Sáenz de Salcedo, Señora de Ayala Linaje de Ayala, descendiente de las Casas Reales de Navarra, de Aragón y de León y de los Señores de Vizcayaとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Conde Pedro Vélez, VII Señor de Guevara Linaje de Vela, Conde de Alava, descendiente de la Casa Real de Navarra, de los Duques de Vasconia, de Aquitana y de Bretaña(이)가 1157년 스페인 우스카디 a라바에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Juan Velaz de Guevara(은)는 17세였고, 그의 어머니 María Álvarez de Arazuri(은)는 23세였다. 그는 María Sáenz de Salcedo, Señora de Ayala Linaje de Ayala, descendiente de las Casas Reales de Navarra, de Aragón y de León y de los Señores de Vizcaya(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Conde Pedro Vélez, VII Señor de Guevara Linaje de Vela, Conde de Alava, descendiente de la Casa Real de Navarra, de los Duques de Vasconia, de Aquitana y de Bretaña nasceu em 1157, em Álava, Euskadi, Espanha, seu pai, Juan Velaz de Guevara, tinha 17 anos e sua mãe, María Álvarez de Arazuri, tinha 23 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com María Sáenz de Salcedo, Señora de Ayala Linaje de Ayala, descendiente de las Casas Reales de Navarra, de Aragón y de León y de los Señores de Vizcaya.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Conde Pedro Vélez, VII Señor de Guevara Linaje de Vela, Conde de Alava, descendiente de la Casa Real de Navarra, de los Duques de Vasconia, de Aquitana y de Bretaña родился в 1157 г. в местности aлава, эускади, Испания, его отцу по имени Juan Velaz de Guevara было 17 лет, а матери, которую звали María Álvarez de Arazuri, 23. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от María Sáenz de Salcedo, Señora de Ayala Linaje de Ayala, descendiente de las Casas Reales de Navarra, de Aragón y de León y de los Señores de Vizcaya.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Conde Pedro Vélez, VII Señor de Guevara Linaje de Vela, Conde de Alava, descendiente de la Casa Real de Navarra, de los Duques de Vasconia, de Aquitana y de Bretaña nació en 1157, en Álava, País Vasco, España, su papá, Juan Velaz de Guevara, tenía 17 años y su mamá, María Álvarez de Arazuri, tenía 23 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con María Sáenz de Salcedo, Señora de Ayala Linaje de Ayala, descendiente de las Casas Reales de Navarra, de Aragón y de León y de los Señores de Vizcaya.
